description_long: This object is a detailed model of a NASA Space Shuttle orbiter, featuring a white fuselage with black thermal protection tiles along the nose, wing edges, and tail. The wings are delta-shaped and mid-mounted, with a slight upward dihedral and no visible winglets. The rear of the shuttle displays three large main engines and two smaller orbital maneuvering system (OMS) pods flanking the vertical stabilizer. The tail is a single, tall vertical stabilizer with a rudder, and the horizontal stabilizers are mounted low on the tail. The cockpit is located at the forward end, with multiple windows arranged in a distinctive shuttle pattern. NASA insignia and other markings are visible on the fuselage, contributing to its authentic appearance.
description: A NASA Space Shuttle orbiter with a white and black color scheme, delta wings, and three large rear engines. The cockpit is forward-mounted with multiple windows, and NASA markings are visible.
description_view_0: Front view showing the shuttle's nose, cockpit windows, and the broad delta wings extending outward.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the length of the fuselage, the forward cockpit, and the vertical stabilizer at the rear.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the three main engines, OMS pods, and the full span of the delta wings.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the shuttle's profile, wing structure, and tail configuration.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Mid-wing delta configuration with slight upward dihedral and no winglets; wings are sharply swept back.
attribute_engine_type: Three rear-mounted jet engines with two additional OMS pods flanking the vertical stabilizer.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single tall vertical stabilizer and low-mounted horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Retractable tricycle landing gear; gear doors visible but gear not deployed.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed multi-seat cockpit with a distinctive array of windows.
